steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The prompt requires emphasizing when Nadia Singh won the award. The notes give exactly one date: 2011. The winning choice must (1) include that date and (2) structure the sentence so the date is prominent.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Nadia Singh is one winner of the A.M. Turing Award.: No date at all, so timing is not emphasized. ✗
  • Choice B — It was in 2011 that Nadia Singh won the A.M. Turing Award.: The cleft construction places 2011 in the most emphatic position in the sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — The prestigious A.M. Turing Award is given for “major contributions of lasting importance to computing.”: No date and no mention of Nadia Singh, so it cannot emphasize timing. ✗
  • Choice D — For developing a calculus for probabilistic reasoning, Nadia Singh won the A.M. Turing Award.: This emphasizes why Singh won, not when — and omits the date entirely. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
